title: Explain the difference between Thread and Runnable
description: Describe the differences between extending Thread class and implementing Runnable interface, including when to use each approach.

title: What is thread synchronization?
description: Explain the concept of thread synchronization in Java and why it's necessary for concurrent programming.

title: Explain the synchronized keyword
description: Describe how the synchronized keyword works for methods and blocks, including its impact on thread execution.

title: What are thread states in Java?
description: List and explain all thread states (NEW, RUNNABLE, BLOCKED, WAITING, TIMED_WAITING, TERMINATED) and transitions between them.

title: Explain wait(), notify(), and notifyAll()
description: Describe the purpose and usage of wait(), notify(), and notifyAll() methods for inter-thread communication.
hints: Must be called within synchronized context

title: What is the volatile keyword?
description: Explain how the volatile keyword ensures visibility of variable changes across threads.

title: Difference between sleep() and wait()
description: Compare Thread.sleep() and Object.wait() methods in terms of lock release and usage context.

title: What is a daemon thread?
description: Explain daemon threads, how they differ from user threads, and when to use them.

title: Explain thread priority
description: Describe how thread priorities work in Java and their impact on thread scheduling.

title: What is the join() method?
description: Explain the purpose of Thread.join() and how it's used to wait for thread completion.

title: Implement a thread-safe singleton using double-checked locking
description: Create a thread-safe singleton pattern using double-checked locking with proper use of volatile keyword.

title: Explain deadlock and how to prevent it
description: Describe what causes deadlock in multithreaded programs and strategies to prevent or detect it.

title: Implement Producer-Consumer pattern
description: Design a producer-consumer solution using wait/notify or BlockingQueue for thread-safe data exchange.

title: Explain ReentrantLock vs synchronized
description: Compare ReentrantLock with synchronized keyword in terms of features, flexibility, and use cases.

title: What is ThreadLocal and when to use it?
description: Explain ThreadLocal variables, their use cases, and potential memory leak issues.

title: Implement a thread pool using ExecutorService
description: Create a thread pool using ExecutorService to manage concurrent task execution efficiently.

title: Explain CountDownLatch and CyclicBarrier
description: Compare CountDownLatch and CyclicBarrier synchronization aids, including their use cases and differences.

title: What is the Fork/Join framework?
description: Explain the Fork/Join framework for parallel task decomposition and work-stealing algorithm.

title: Implement a thread-safe counter
description: Create a thread-safe counter using different approaches: synchronized, AtomicInteger, and locks.

title: Explain Semaphore and its use cases
description: Describe how Semaphore works for controlling access to resources and implementing resource pools.

title: Implement a custom ReadWriteLock
description: Design and implement a ReadWriteLock that allows multiple readers or a single writer with proper fairness.

title: Explain the Java Memory Model and happens-before
description: Describe the Java Memory Model, happens-before relationships, and how they guarantee memory visibility.

title: Implement a lock-free data structure
description: Create a lock-free stack or queue using compare-and-swap (CAS) operations and atomic references.

title: Design a custom thread pool with work stealing
description: Implement a thread pool with work-stealing algorithm for load balancing across worker threads.

title: Implement a concurrent LRU cache
description: Design a thread-safe LRU cache with O(1) operations using fine-grained locking or lock-free techniques.

title: Explain StampedLock and optimistic locking
description: Describe StampedLock's optimistic read mode and when it provides better performance than ReadWriteLock.

title: Implement a Phaser for multi-phase synchronization
description: Use Phaser to coordinate multiple phases of parallel computation with dynamic thread registration.

title: Design a rate limiter with thread safety
description: Implement a thread-safe rate limiter using token bucket or sliding window algorithm.

title: Explain false sharing and cache line padding
description: Describe false sharing problem in concurrent programs and how to prevent it using cache line padding.

title: Implement a concurrent skip list
description: Design a lock-free or fine-grained locking skip list for concurrent sorted data structure operations.
